article_title: CRISPR Therapeutics and Vertex post $43 million CASGEVY revenue in Q1 2026 as pediatric submission lands with FDA Priority Voucher

content_preview.title: CRISPR Therapeutics and Vertex post $43 million CASGEVY revenue in Q1 2026 as pediatric submission lands with FDA Priority Voucher
content_preview.summary: First-quarter results from both partners show CASGEVY past 500 treatment starts, a US filing for ages 5 to under 12, and CRISPR Therapeutics' in vivo pipeline advancing across four liver-directed programs.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

CRISPR Therapeutics and Vertex Pharmaceuticals each reported first-quarter 2026 financial results within 24 hours of one another, both attributing $43 million in revenue to CASGEVY, the gene-edited therapy the two companies co-developed for severe sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia. The matching figure, disclosed by CRISPR Therapeutics on May 4 according to [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/05/04/3287176/0/en/CRISPR-Therapeut...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong News piece, ~819 words. Tight financial reporting structure (CASGEVY revenue, regulatory progress, in vivo pipeline, Q1 financials, Vertex guidance). Internal cross-reference to April 25 Intellia article verified on disk.

source_verification.source-0_globenewswire: Verified verbatim: $43M Q1 CASGEVY revenue, 500+ patients initiated, 60,000 eligible / 37,000 NA+EU / 23,000 ME, Net loss $122.9M (vs $136.0M), cash $2,441.8M / $1,975.8M, $585.4M convertible note proceeds, Phase 1b CTX310 (sHTG/refractory hypercholesterolemia), CTX340/460/321 IND-enabling framing, 'two-fold greater potency' for CTX321, zugo-cel/CTX112 14+ patients across SSc/IM/SLE, 'remained in DORIS remission through Month 12 and Month 6', Kulkarni quote, Commissioner's National Priority Voucher.
source_verification.source-1_biospace_crispr: BioSpace mirror of CRISPR Therapeutics release. Same content as source-0, no new claims.
source_verification.source-2_biospace_vertex: Verified verbatim: total Q1 revenue $2.99 billion / 8% growth, FY2026 guidance $12.95-13.1B and $0.5B+ non-CF, Kewalramani quote 'CASGEVY and JOURNAVX delivered more than 25 percent of our growth' (the article uses Kewalramani's separate 'off to a strong start in 2026' framing which also appears in the release), 5 to less than 12 pediatric submission, Germany pricing agreement.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quoted material traced verbatim. Numerical claims (revenues, patient counts, financials, pipeline patient counts, remission durations) all verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and disciplined biotech-financial reporting. The 'What we don't know' section is honest about geography breakdown gaps and the absence of a target action date.
editor_notes.originality: Builds on prior April 25 Intellia article with proper internal cross-reference. Distinct angle (CRISPR Therapeutics + Vertex Q1 dual report).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Cryptography, sources, content, and quoting discipline all clean.
